resposta:
```python
class Solution:
    def bitwiseComplement(self, n: int) -> int:
        if n == 0:
            return 1
        mask = n
        mask |= mask >> 1
        mask |= mask >> 2
        mask |= mask >> 4
        mask |= mask >> 8
        mask |= mask >> 16
        return n ^ mask
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses bitwise operations to efficiently calculate the complement of the given integer. It first creates a mask with all bits set to the right of the most significant bit in `n`, then uses the XOR operation to flip the bits in `n`. This approach is efficient and has a constant time complexity.
